title: erythroxylum riparium
intro: Erythroxylum riparium, also known as the river coca plant, is a species of shrub in the Erythroxylum genus, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Erythroxylum riparium include reducing inflammation and pain, as well as providing antioxidant protection against oxidative stress and cell damage. The herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are typically used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and respiratory infections.
